mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off positioning and reduces equity valuations over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in residential housing starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the combined proxy correlation drops below 0.5 during peak market stress.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which improves profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns when inflation remains contained.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL increases exceeding 0.4% month-over-month, indicating supply-driven gains are offset by inflation shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX 60-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling correlation between GDP revisions and index returns falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3% signals accelerating price pressures that force market participants to price in tighter monetary policy, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally for 5 consecutive days following inflation prints above 0.3% in more than 40% of cases, or the signal shows no statistical edge against a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous non-farm payroll growth exceeding 150,000 and a stable unemployment rate below 4.5% confirms a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal predicts positive returns in fewer than 45% of instances when payroll growth falls below 100,000, or the interaction term loses significance when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts indicate a demand shock that reduces near-term corporate revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns remain positive despite concurrent declines in housing starts in more than 50% of historical episodes, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during recessionary periods.
